channel.title: Posts on JF
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on JF

item.title: How to automatically translate all Hugo blog entries with AI
item.description:
I assume you followed the Howto create a hugo blog guide

MY QUESTION

I have a Hugo blog with a lot of blog entries index.md in different subdirectories in the folder content/original/posts/postname/index.md. What i want to do now is to automatically either translate or enhance the posts with AI. First, I copy everything from content/original/posts/postname/ to content/en/posts/postname and then I want to create an api call to some chat AI that automatically translates each blog post index.md and saves this blog post in content/en/posts/postname/index.md. If same language, just enhance the text by AI. But I also have to ensure that in code blocks only the comments are translated and links to images in the content/en/posts/postname/images folder or websites stay the same etc. Help me do that.

item.title: Use Neoylayout
item.description:
This is a short summary of the essentials of the keybord layout neo-layout variant bone for faster and healtier text (and math) typing on your PC.

Windows

To use it on Windows follow these steps.

Install

  • Copy the kbdbone.dll file in System32 to C:\Windows\System32 and the one in SysWOW64 to C:\Windows\SysWOW64 respectively. Grant access rights. (On 64-bit Windows you have to copy it with a 64-bit Programm, best by the Windows Explorer)

item.title: OOBE
item.description:
What is OOBE?

OOBE is the Windows Out of Box Experience, that you have to click through after a fresh installation of Windows on your Computer. There you can set the system language, keyboard layout, connect to the internet and

Problem 1: Windows wants to force you to use a Microsoft Account

Microsoft wants you to use a Microsoft Account that is connected to their Cloud - to use all the amazing online Microsoft Features (and conveniently collect your data. Mpf). But instead of adding a Button “Use local account instead” they lay stones in your path. What a surprise! Solution 1 helps

item.title: SQL
item.description:
This post is my personal SQL Cheat Sheet, based on the SQL tutorial by W3Schools .

tl;dr SQL let’s you access and manipulate databases.

Why SQL

SQL can execute queries, retrieve data, insert records, update records, delete record to/from a database as well as create/delete a database, create/delete tables in a database, create views and stored procedures in a database and finally set permissions on tables, procedures and views.

Many database programs build on SQL, but add some (proprietary) functionalities.

item.title: Conda
item.description:
Why Conda?

Conda is an environment and package management system, widely used in scientific computing and data science. Very handy if you program in Python. Conda features:

  • Cross-Platform Compatibility
  • Package Management
  • Environment Management (you can switch easily)
  • Version control
  • Dependency Management

The handy thing about conda is that you can create a new environment for each project that you have. Then you install all the packages for each project in it’s own dedicated environment. Then you can swap back and forth to another project that might need totally different packages and versions. The different packages and versions are kind of ‘containerized’ and don’t interfer with each other.

item.title: Git Delete Commit History
item.description:
There are actually two possible methods, I recommend method 1.

Method 1

Create a new temporary orphan branch, delete the main branch and rename the temporary branch to main.

git pull origin main # Make sure your on the last version
git checkout --orphan temporary_branch # Create new branch
git add -A # Add all current content to
git commit -am "Initial commit" # Commit all content to temporary_branch
git branch -D main # Remove main branch
git branch -m main # Rename current temporary_branch to main
git push -f origin main # Force push to main
git remote prune origin # Prune old associations

All done!

item.title: Custom Anki Card
item.description: Today, I came accross my old Notes from my German Class at School. I decided to learn the stylistic devices (dt. “Stilmittel”) again. To do so, I want to use Anki and create a custom card with the three fields Name, Definition and Example. In this guide, I will explain how to create a custom Anki card (template) with multiple fields and cross-learning. When the cards are created, I have to learn each three times.

item.title: Git
item.description:
Git Basics

Find Version

(Often used to test if git is installed)

git --version

Set username and email address

git config --global user.name "jokobus"
git config --global user.email "jokobus@example.com"

If you want to use the username only for the git repository of the current directory, remove the global option.

If you want to see which username and email is currently set, use

git config --global user.name
git config --global user.email

If you are using a credential manager, you might need to clear the stored credentials

item.title: Java
item.description:
This is my personal Java Summary. There’s nothing special about it, I just published it, because I already have it in markdown format, so why not?

A huge Thank You to https://www.w3schools.com/java/java_scope.asp . Most examples are more or less directly from the public Java Tutorial. Some of the more interesting I copied into my summary.

1. Basics

Compile & Run

C:\Users\Yourname\Path>javac Main.java	// Compile
C:\Users\Yourname\Path>java Main		// Execute

IO

int number = 5;
System.out.println("bla"+number);

Type casting

int x = 5;
double z = x; // Widening cast: z = 5.0, done automatically
int y = (int) z; // Narrowing cast: x = 5, need to specify the cast manually

Strings

String firstname = "Peter ";
String lastname = "Pan";
firstname.toUpperCase(); //PETER
firstname.toLowerCase(); //peter
firstname.length(); // 5
String name = firstname + lastname; // Peter Pan, or
String name = firstname.concat(lastname); // Peter Pan

String <-> Integer Conversion

String mystring = String.valueOf(myint); // if int myint = 5; -> mystring = "5"
int myint = Integer.valueOf(mystring); // if mystring = "10"; -> myint = 10;

Math helpers

Math.max(myint); Math.max(5,10); //10
Math.min(myint);
Math.abs(myint);
Math.sqrt(myint);
int randomNum = (int)(Math.random() * 101); // 0 to 100

2. Conditions & Loops

If, else, elseif

if, else, else if as in C

item.title: How to include Scalable Vector Graphics (svg) in $\LaTeX$
item.description:
You may know the popular LaTeX package graphicx which you can use as:

\includegraphics[options]{filename}

graphicx does not support svg, but there is the package called svg that does the job.

How to use the svg package:

  1. Install Inkscape

    On Windows you can use winget.

    winget install -e --id Inkscape.Inkscape

  2. Copy your image.svg file into your document root folder (= right next to your .tex file)

  3. This is a MWE for your .tex file:

    \documentclass{article}
\includepackage{svg}
\begin{document}
 \begin{figure}
 \includesvg[]{image}
 \end{figure}
\end{document}

Notes:

item.title: LBA Kompetenznachweis
item.description:
Achtung Disclaimer Dies sind meine persönlichen Notizen zum LBA Kompetenznachweis, der in Europa zum Führen von unbemannten Luftfahrzeugen (v.a. im Hobby-Bereich) berechtigt. Es besteht kein Anspruch auf Richtigkeit. Alle Informationen - soweit nicht anderweitig gekennzeichnet - stammen von https://lba-openuav.de/onlinekurs/ .

Checkliste

  • Kompetenznachweis (Drohnenführerschein)
  • Betreiberregistrierung
  • Versicherung
  • Checke, dass du mit der richtigen Drohnenklasse in der richtigen Kategorie unterwegs bist

Der einfachste Weg, um legal loslegen zu können ist, die Website lba-openuav.de zu besuchen, dort den Online-Kurs zu machen, den A1/A3 (kleinen) Drohnenführerschein und sich dort nach bestehen des kurzen Tests gleich noch als UAV-Betreiber eintragen. Zuletzt noch kurz checken, dass die Haftpflichtversicherung Drohnen mit abdeckt und los kann es gehen.

item.title: How to solve git VPN issue
item.description:
If you try to use git you can get the following error:

fatal: unable to access 'https://github.com/yourusername/yourrepository.git/': Could not resolve host: github.com

This error can happen, if you are using a VPN.

The solution is to either toggle your VPN and try again or (if you don’t want to toggle your VPN) to execute:

  • If your VPN is currently OFF:

    git config --global --unset http.proxy

  • If your VPN is currently ON:

item.title: How to create a blog with Hugo (full guide)
item.description:
This is a short guide how to create a Hugo blog, using the tailwind theme, which is the one I’m currently using myself. This guide was written with unexperienced users in mind. If you follow this guide 1:1 you should get a running instance (no guarantee of course). If you are confident with the topic, you can use this guide to get inspired and modify the steps to your own preference.

Although the following steps are for Windows, the whole process is the same for Linux/MacOS. Only some of the console commands are slightly different. E.g. instead of the powershell command Start-process you can use nano or vim and instead of winget you may use your distribution’s package manager or homebrew.

item.title: Linear Regression
item.description:
Overview

Why and when do we need linear regression? In a nutshell, linear regression is used to predict data that has a linear relationship. That means all the datapoints are roughly on a straight line (in 2D), resp. on a hyperplane (in 3D). Firstly, we find some expression for the linear relationship (e.g. in 2D: $y=a\cdot x + b$; where $a$ is the slope and $b$ is the y-intercept). Secondly, we use this expression to insert some data where we only know the $x$ value, but want to predict their unknown $y$ value.

item.title: Feedforward Phase
item.description:
Introduction

This article gives a quick overview over the feedforward phase of a Neural Network.

Nodes

A Neural Network is made of many Nodes that are connected to each other. Each Node itself is a very basic unit that does only do three things.

  1. Multiply each input with a weight and sum all inputs up. $y_{in} = \sum_{i=0}^m w_i\cdot x_i$, where $m$ is the number of inputs.
  2. Process the single, scalar input value by some activation function $y_{out}=f(y_{in})$ where $y_{in}$ is the result from step 1 and $y_{out}$ is the result from step 1 after being applied to the activation function.
  3. Output the result $y_{out}$ to the next node

So, a single node pretty much looks like this:

Figure 1: A single Node with its weights
